tierDefinitions.3: Extrapolation (beyond reliable ephemeris range, or date-only entries)
tierDefinitions.1A: Modern direct observation: < 1 arcsec accuracy (LLR, modern eclipse timing, radar)
tierDefinitions.1B: Telescope-era observation: 1-40 arcsec accuracy (transit contact times, Flamsteed)
tierDefinitions.1C: Pre-telescope precision observation: 1-2 arcmin accuracy (Tycho Brahe)

weightDefinitions.0: Tier 3: for comparison only, not used in optimization
weightDefinitions.1: Tier 2: modern ephemeris reference (computed, not observed)

notes: ALL position values (dec, ra, longitude) are currently Tier 2 (computed from ephemeris)
Transit/opposition DATES may be Tier 1 (historically observed) but positions are still Tier 2
Future Tier 1 data: observed transit contact times, eclipse records, historical measurements
JPL enrichment adds RA values where only dec is available (still Tier 2)

jplEnrichment.source: JPL Horizons API (geocentric, astrometric RA/Dec in decimal degrees)
